assertions: Assistant output contains the refusal sentence `Eval System is not initialised. Run `/z-eval-init` first to create `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml.` verbatim when `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is absent.
Assistant output does not include a delegated `/canvas` dataset package or comparative row tables until the initialise gate passes.

primitive_analysis.summary: `/z-eval-compare` rejects uninitialised Eval System runs, resolves each operand to `_runs/*/report.yml` (with rollup context from sibling YAML and `report.static` / `report.llm`), and either ships a scoped `/canvas` JSON hand-off with template-backed instructions or replays verbatim `needs_user_input` when fragments stay ambiguous—all without invoking `askQuestion` for operand disambiguation.

cases.expected_output: The palette command stops immediately with guidance to initialise Eval System rather than spawning compare tooling or probing `_runs/` directories.

assertions: Assistant narrative confirms each slash argument was threaded through untouched (including alphanumeric fragments embedded in timestamps) before invoking compare mode.
After `/z-eval-compare`, assistant output references reading each operand’s primary `report.yml` plus `report.static` and `report.llm` rollup blocks when consolidating totals.
Dataset rows carry identifiers for `run_id`, `case_id`, `status`, comparative metrics fields, models, and a `log_path` pointer into `*_runs/*/logs/`.
Assistant emits a `{ "tool": "/canvas", "instructions": [...], "dataset": [...] }` structure and directs the operator to call `/canvas` for rendering.
`instructions[]` duplicates the bytes loaded from `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/canvas/compare-prompt.md.tmpl` file-for-file with the repository template.

primitive_analysis.summary: `/z-eval-compare` rejects uninitialised Eval System runs, resolves each operand to `_runs/*/report.yml` (with rollup context from sibling YAML and `report.static` / `report.llm`), and either ships a scoped `/canvas` JSON hand-off with template-backed instructions or replays verbatim `needs_user_input` when fragments stay ambiguous—all without invoking `askQuestion` for operand disambiguation.

cases.expected_output: The assistant produces a flattened cross-run table covering every supplied operand plus instructions that route charting through `/canvas` using the templated narration file.

assertions: When multiple hourly folders match fragments, assistant output replays structured `needs_user_input` verbatim (YAML or JSON) exposing every ambiguous candidate beneath `*_runs/` with no summarised downsampling of entries.
Assistant explains comparisons remain paused until `/z-eval-compare` reruns with disambiguated `_runs/` identifiers.
No command-owned `AskQuestion` / `askQuestion` prompts appear solely to differentiate `_runs/` folders mid-flight.
Assistant never instructs chaining `resume` to clear the ambiguity; it insists on rerun with clarified operands instead.

cases.expected_output: Operators see a deterministic structured blockage listing overlapping `_runs/` matches so they can rerun with unique basenames, not an interactive narrowing dialog.

assertions: Flattened dataset narrative preserves rollup slices sourced from sibling `static.yml` / `llm.yml` when judge-facing rows still exist after upstream `/z-eval-judge`.
Flattened totals carry judge-tier columns such as `accuracy`, `confidence`, or equivalent metrics enumerated in reporting YAML without dropping them relative to backing `report.yml` excerpts.

cases.expected_output: The assistant keeps judge-enriched rollup columns coherent while flattening so `/canvas` rows stay faithful to adjudicated artefacts.
